title: Cooling Coil Rating Parameters
summary: Standard rating parameters for extended-surface air-cooling and dehumidifying coils per AHRI Standard 410.

title: Coil Air Face Velocity Range
summary: Standard air face velocity range for coil ratings.

title: Chilled-Water Temperature Range
summary: Standard entering chilled-water temperature range for coil ratings.

title: Water Velocity Range for Coils
summary: Standard water velocity range for coil ratings.

title: Coil Sensible Heat Ratio Range
summary: Typical range of sensible to total heat ratio for dehumidifying coils in practice.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Sensible to total heat ratio range

evidence_quote: The air-side ratio of sensible to total heat removed by dehumidifying coils varies in practice from about 0.6 to 1.0

title: Dehumidifying Coil Velocity Selection
summary: Recommended air face velocity range for selecting dehumidifying coils in comfort applications.
evidence_quote: Dehumidifying coils for comfort applications are frequently selected in the range of 400 to 500 fpm air face velocity.

title: Standard Air Density for Ratings
summary: Standard air density used for airflow ratings of coils.
evidence_quote: Airflow ratings are based on standard air of 0.075 lb/ft3 at 70°F and a barometric pressure of 29.92 in. Hg.

title: Coil Freezing Prevention
summary: Condition for freezing of dehumidifying coil surface and typical refrigerant temperature threshold.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Refrigerant evaporating temperature threshold for freezing

evidence_quote: Freezing does not occur with standard coils for comfort installations unless the refrigerant evaporating temperature at the coil outlet is below 25 to 28°F saturated

title: Coil Airflow Resistance Comparison
summary: Comparison of airflow resistance between a dry sensible cooling coil and a wet dehumidifying coil.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Airflow resistance reduction for dry coil

evidence_quote: A completely dry coil, removing only sensible heat, offers approximately one-third less resistance to airflow than a dehumidifying coil removing both sensible and latent heat.

title: Hydrophilic Coating Pressure Drop Reduction
summary: Measured reduction in air pressure drop across a hydrophilic coil versus a new untreated coil.
evidence_quote: Tests have shown as much as a 30% reduction in air pressure drop across a hydrophilic coil as opposed a new untreated coil.
